title: Mixed-color merge grouped by destination
summary: Fibers from different tubes or cables turn vertical and converge onto a shared horizontal group of mixed colors. Group membership is defined by shared path and destination, not tube color.
visually_observable_good_behavior: Strands from different source colors merge onto a shared horizontal run.
The merged group is organized by destination fan-in order, not source tube color.
Mixed colors remain nested and readable after the merge.
Disorder at the merge is absorbed without avoidable crossings.

followed_requirements: Groups are defined by shared path and destination.
Members are nested by destination fan-in order.
Lane bands keep related strand lanes adjacent while preserving readable separation.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: Mixed-color strands converge into a destination-defined nested group.

followed_requirements: The routing engine uses available horizontal and vertical space.
Routes remain traceable through merge transitions.
likely_followed_rules.why_this_image_passes: The merge uses center-zone space cleanly without congestion.

legacy_rule_mapping.note: Legacy D10 destination-merge concept maps to SDC-ROUTE-002.

notes: Positive reference for destination-based grouping across tube boundaries.
